hero.platformFocus: This platform focuses on AI computing, new energy thermal management, advanced interface materials, and high-hardness, high-conductivity applications, delivering breakthrough thermal management solutions for high-power-density electronic devices.

techEvolution.title: Technology Evolution Path
techEvolution.desc: Based on the same underlying platform, we are extending toward advanced packaging thermal solutions, solid-state battery skeletons, and beyond.

features.innovationAction: Reject traditional physical blending; adopt atomic-level covalent bonding
features.innovationEffect: Enable precise control of internal stress fields and energy-level environments, delivering a material platform that pushes beyond limits for AI and new energy thermal management
features.patentsLogic: Preparation process, bonding structure, stress control, and functional extension
features.patentsStatus: Nearly 10 core patents deployed; full IP closed-loop in place
features.patentsValue: Solid technical boundaries and legal safeguards for product iteration and application expansion
features.industrialStatus: From lab hypothesis to process closure; samples have completed engineering-level thermal validation
features.industrialMode: Strategic licensing and joint development open
features.industrialVision: Partner with long-cycle strategic allies to define the physical boundaries of next-gen thermal management materials

platform.title: Platform Positioning & Value
platform.p1: In high-power-density electronic devices, AI computing, and new energy systems, traditional materials have approached physical limits in thermal conductivity, thermal expansion matching, and reliability.
platform.p2: Rather than pursuing single-parameter optimization, this platform provides an extensible material substrate:
platform.li1: Through sp²/sp³ bonding and stress engineering, unify high thermal conductivity with tunable electronic environments
platform.li2: Reserve structural and property interfaces for future thermal interface materials, heat spreaders, and exploratory physical devices
platform.p3: We regard this as a long-term evolvable underlying platform, not a specific end product.

diagram.layer3Label: High power density, CTE mismatch, reliability bottleneck
diagram.platformAttr: Long-term evolvable underlying platform, not a specific end product
diagram.rdConcept: Atomic-level interface fusion ensures property determinism

results.title: From Theory Validation to Sample Delivery
results.subtitle: Our phased results
results.processDesc: Completed full laboratory validation from physical hypothesis → preparation process → performance characterization, with preliminary sample preparation and performance testing underway.
results.deliverablesDesc: Stable diamond-copper composite and superabrasive fusion-bonded tool samples; some samples have completed engineering-level validation of key thermal performance metrics and entered further optimization.
results.ipDesc: Core technology routes are fully self-developed and patented; the technology does not depend on external open frameworks or patchwork solutions, ensuring independence and innovation.

products.title: Product Matrix
products.subtitle: From consumer-grade diamond nonstick coating to cutting-edge all-carbon composites, each technology represents a breakthrough in CuFeng Technology

nonstickCookware.title: Diamond coating nonstick cookware
nonstickCookware.desc: Patented diamond particle bonding technology for ultra-wear resistance and lasting nonstick performance. 10x+ lifetime over traditional coatings; mass production ready.
nonstickCookware.feature1: Brazing-bonded diamond particle coating, permanent bonding
nonstickCookware.feature2: 10x+ wear resistance improvement, significantly extended service life

hero.title: True diamond coating nonstick cookware
hero.intro: Based on patented active fusion-bonding technology, diamond particles achieve atomic-level bonding with the metal substrate. Applying this process to nonstick cookware validates the reliability of interface fixation strength under extreme operating conditions.
hero.feature1: Real diamond particle active fusion bonding—never peels
hero.feature3: Heat resistant to 500°C—suitable for all cooking methods
hero.imgAltSem: Diamond coating nonstick cookware SEM image

whyDiamond.title: Why choose diamond?
whyDiamond.intro: Diamond is the hardest material in nature, with excellent thermal conductivity and chemical stability. We apply these natural advantages to cookware, redefining nonstick performance standards.
whyDiamond.hardnessDesc: Mohs hardness 10; scratch resistance 1000x that of stainless steel
whyDiamond.thermalDesc: Thermal conductivity 2000+ W/m·K; fast, even heating
whyDiamond.inertDesc: No reaction with food; no harmful substance release
whyDiamond.durableDesc: Active fusion bonding, high-strength bonding; 10+ year service life.

keyFeatures.title: Core advantages
keyFeatures.subtitle: Patent technology empowering; redefining nonstick performance standards
keyFeatures.lifespanDesc: Diamond particles are permanently bonded to the substrate via active fusion bonding, forming chemical bonds. 10x+ lifetime over traditional coatings; withstands 100,000+ use cycles, and the coating will not peel off.
keyFeatures.safetyDesc: Diamond material is non-toxic and harmless; coating contains no PFOA/PTFE or other harmful substances. Can pass GB 4806.1-2016 food contact material safety testing; FDA recognition capable. Safe to use.
keyFeatures.thermalDesc: Diamond's ultra-high thermal conductivity (2000+ W/m·K) ensures even, rapid heat distribution and avoids local overheating. Cooking efficiency improved 30%+; food heats more evenly, locking in nutrition and flavor.

comparison.title: Performance comparison: Clear advantages
comparison.subtitle: Diamond coating nonstick vs traditional coated cookware

cta.title: Learn more about diamond nonstick coating technology

diamondCopper.title: Diamond-copper composite
diamondCopper.desc: Achieved 680 W/m·K thermal conductivity; premium version can reach 1200+ W/m·K. Dual product line strategy covering cost-effective civilian and high-end applications.
diamondCopper.feature2: Premium performance version: 1200+ W/m·K, CVD alternative
diamondCopper.feature3: AI chips, power devices, and other high-end thermal applications

hero.title: Diamond-Copper Composite
hero.intro: Core product of our thermal management technology pivot. We have successfully developed samples with thermal conductivity of 680 W/m·K and planned two product lines: a cost-effective high-volume civilian solution and a premium version exceeding 1200 W/m·K thermal conductivity, which can replace expensive CVD diamond.

applicationProspects.title: Application Prospects
applicationProspects.card1Title: New benchmark for thermal management materials
applicationProspects.card1Desc: Diamond-copper composites are becoming the standard thermal solution for high-power electronic devices. From AI chips to new energy vehicles, from 5G base stations to aerospace, the application prospects are broad.
applicationProspects.card2Desc: The cost-effective version targets high-volume civilian markets; the premium version targets high-end industrial applications. Both product lines develop synergistically, covering the full market from consumer to industrial.

lineA.title: Cost-effective solution
lineA.desc: Low-cost high-volume preparation through process optimization, targeting civilian markets and supporting scaled applications.
lineA.scenario1: Consumer electronics thermal management (phones, tablets, laptops)

lineB.title: Premium performance solution
lineB.desc: Active fusion-bonded multi-grade diamond particle composite, 1200+ W/m·K thermal conductivity, can replace expensive CVD solutions for high-end thermal management needs.
lineB.costAdvantageValue: As low as 1/10 of CVD diamond
lineB.scenario1: AI chips, GPU thermal management (high power density)

technicalDetails.title: Technical Principle & Innovation
technicalDetails.skeletonDesc: Using multi-grade particle size gradient technology, titanium-coated diamond particles build a self-supporting 3D continuous skeleton. Through precise packing optimization, the diamond phase achieves very high volume fraction, forming interconnected high-speed phonon transport channels.
technicalDetails.gradingLogicDesc: Large particles build the main framework; medium and small particles achieve stepwise precise filling of micron-scale voids.
technicalDetails.interfaceModificationDesc: Particle surface pretreatment ensures extremely high self-support strength and interface thermal conductivity of the skeleton.
technicalDetails.tospikeDesc: Relying on our self-developed low-melting-point active fusion-bonding alloy system, we have broken through the wettability bottleneck of traditional diamond composites. This technology allows the alloy liquid phase to spontaneously penetrate deep into the complex skeleton with excellent fluidity, building a near-perfect heat transfer network.
technicalDetails.techAdvantageDesc: Mild process conditions effectively avoid thermal damage to the diamond surface.
technicalDetails.interfaceStrengthDesc: Chemical bonding under pressure assistance endows the material with excellent mechanical properties and thermal stability.
technicalDetails.step1Desc: Titanium-coated diamond particles are filled into the mold in large, medium, and small order; vibration achieves dense packing to form a 3D continuous porous skeleton.
technicalDetails.step2Desc: Active fusion alloy powder is vibration-filled into the pores of the diamond skeleton to ensure uniform distribution.
technicalDetails.step3Desc: Heat in a vacuum hot-press furnace above the alloy liquidus, apply pressure and hold. Active elements synergistically form composite carbide interfaces, achieving reliable bonding between diamond and metal matrix.
technicalDetails.step4Desc: Cool with the furnace to solidify, forming a fully dense diamond-copper composite structure. Can be further wire-cut to required dimensions.

patents.subtitle: Complete intellectual property protection system
patents.patent1Title: A diamond-copper composite material and its preparation method
patents.patent1Desc: Discloses a preparation method using multi-grade diamond particle packing to build skeleton and active fusion liquid phase filling. Achieves high thermal conductivity, low cost, and large-size preparation.
patents.patent2Title: A structured diamond thermal management substrate and its preparation method
patents.patent2Desc: Describes in detail key technologies including large, medium, and small diamond particle grading, titanium coating, coating process, and positioning screen-assisted filling.

cta.title: Need diamond-copper thermal solutions?
cta.subtitle: Our technical team will provide optimal product selection and customized design services based on your thermal requirements

thermalPad.title: Thermal interface material (TIM)
thermalPad.desc: Flexible thermal solution based on copper mesh/copper fiber. Active fusion bonding of diamond particles forms a 3D skeleton conductive phase with high thermal conductivity and flexibility.
thermalPad.feature2: Active fusion bonding of high thermal conductivity particles
thermalPad.feature3: Adapts to complex interface flexible thermal conduction

hero.title: Flexible thermal pad
hero.intro: Innovative flexible thermal solution based on copper mesh/copper fiber 3D skeleton. Through active fusion-bonding technology, high thermal conductivity particles such as diamond are fixed to a flexible metal skeleton, forming a 3D thermal conductive phase network with both high thermal conductivity and excellent interface adaptability.

techAdvantages.title: Technical advantages
techAdvantages.card1Desc: Uses copper mesh or copper fiber as 3D skeleton, providing continuous thermal conduction channels. The skeleton itself has good flexibility, adapting to uneven interfaces and thermal stress deformation.
techAdvantages.card2Desc: Through active fusion-bonding technology, high thermal conductivity particles such as diamond and boron nitride are firmly bonded to the metal skeleton, forming stable chemical metallurgical connections for long-term reliability.
techAdvantages.card3Desc: Breaks through limitations of traditional rigid thermal materials; while maintaining high thermal conductivity, provides excellent flexibility and interface adaptability, solving thermal conduction challenges in complex assembly scenarios.

technicalDetails.title: Technical principle & preparation
technicalDetails.skeletonDesc: Uses precision micron-scale metal mesh or directionally aligned metal fibers as 3D thermal conduction scaffold. Leverages metal's excellent intrinsic thermal conductivity and good mechanical ductility to build continuous heat conduction network.
technicalDetails.skeletonItem1: Mesh structure: Regular geometric arrangement, precise thickness control, ensuring consistency for large-area applications.
technicalDetails.skeletonItem2: Fiber network: 3D disordered interweaving, excellent mechanical flexibility, perfectly adapting to non-flat interfaces.
technicalDetails.enhancementDesc: Within microscopic pores of the metal skeleton, precisely embed modified carbon-based reinforcement particles (e.g., diamond, boron nitride). Through interface affinity engineering, achieve atomic-level metallurgical bonding between reinforcement and metal skeleton, eliminating physical gaps in traditional materials.
technicalDetails.diamondEnhancementDesc: Provides rapid vertical thermal conduction channels
technicalDetails.ceramicEnhancementDesc: Balances high thermal conductivity with system electrical insulation needs
technicalDetails.step1Desc: Deep cleaning and surface energy regulation of metal skeleton, removing microscopic oxide layers. Through self-developed surface modification process, significantly improves interface wettability and bonding strength in subsequent brazing.
technicalDetails.step2Desc: Uniformly disperse specially coated reinforcement particles and active interface components; using vacuum-assisted or high-frequency physical energy, fill the 3D space of the skeleton to build gap-free thermal paths.
technicalDetails.step3Desc: Under vacuum or precisely controlled protective atmosphere, through optimized thermal cycle, induce active elements to undergo in-situ chemical reaction at the interface. Process completes under low pressure, achieving stable carbide/nitride interface formation, eliminating interface thermal resistance.
technicalDetails.step4Desc: Release internal stress through controlled cooling logic to obtain overall flexible structure with excellent performance. Can undergo surface flattening or precision cutting per design drawings based on end requirements.

applicationProspects.title: Application prospects
applicationProspects.card1Title: Evolution of high thermal conductivity interface materials
applicationProspects.card1Desc: This solution combines metal strength, carbon-based rapid heat transfer, and polymer compliance. As next-gen high-performance thermal interface material (TIM), it addresses traditional thermal pad issues of "low thermal conductivity, easy drying/cracking, poor interface conformity," widely applied in:
applicationProspects.card1Item1: New energy powertrain: Ensures long-term thermal safety of battery packs and motor controllers.
applicationProspects.card1Item2: High-integration comm base stations: Addresses extreme heat flux density of 5G/6G RF modules.
applicationProspects.card1Item3: Space-grade electronic payloads: Achieves ultimate heat spreading under lightweight requirements.
applicationProspects.card2Desc: We provide full-chain R&D services from skeleton design, particle formulation optimization to finished product specification customization. For customer-specific heat flux density needs, through interface stress and reinforcement distribution adjustment, we provide unique performance customization.

patents.title: Related patent technology
patents.subtitle: Core intellectual property layout
patents.intro: We have built a solid patent moat covering material composition, interface engineering, and core preparation processes.
patents.patentTitle: A flexible high-thermal-conductivity composite reinforced by active fusion-bonding metal skeleton and its preparation method
patents.patentDesc: This patent defines in detail a systematic method using metal mesh/fiber as structural base and in-situ metallurgical welding to fix high thermal conductivity particles. Its core value is replacing traditional physical doping with interface chemical bonding, fundamentally improving interface heat transfer efficiency and anti-aging reliability.

hero.title: All-carbon composite
hero.intro: Revolutionary all-carbon composite technology platform based on sp²–sp³ chemical bonding. Using nanodiamond surface graphitization as "brazing material," achieves chemical bonding between diamond (sp³) and graphite/carbon fiber (sp²), building a 3D all-carbon network structure. Extensible to thermal management, solid-state batteries, superconducting materials, and other frontier fields.

coreTech.title: sp²–sp³ core technology principle
coreTech.subtitle: Breaking through physical contact limits of traditional material interfaces; achieving atomic-level connection between carbon materials through chemical bonding
coreTech.graphitizationDesc: Under inert or reducing atmosphere, nanodiamond surface (1–3 nm shell) can undergo graphitization, forming a thin graphitized "soft shell." This shell has sp² hybridization, enabling covalent bonding with sp² structures of other carbon materials.
coreTech.bondingDesc: sp² carbon atoms in the graphitized shell undergo carbon atom diffusion and rearrangement with graphene, carbon fiber, carbon nanotubes and other sp² carbon materials at high temperature, forming C–C covalent bonds for atomic-level chemical bonding between diamond and carbon materials.
coreTech.networkDesc: Through rational design of diamond particle and sp² carbon material ratio and spatial distribution, builds interconnected sp²–sp³ 3D carbon network structure, combining diamond's high hardness and thermal conductivity with graphite/carbon fiber's electrical conductivity and flexibility.
coreTech.allCarbonDesc: No metallic phase; avoids metal thermal expansion mismatch, electrochemical corrosion. Lightweight, high strength, excellent chemical stability.
coreTech.chemicalBondingDesc: C–C covalent bonding; interface bonding strength far exceeds physical contact or weak van der Waals. Continuous thermal and electrical conduction channels; no interface thermal/electrical resistance.
coreTech.tunableDesc: By adjusting diamond particle size, content, sp² material type (graphene, carbon fiber, nanotubes), thermal conductivity, electrical conductivity, and mechanical properties can be flexibly customized.
coreTech.multifunctionDesc: Same technical principle extensible to multiple fields: high thermal conductivity materials, solid-state battery anodes, particle electrodes, superconducting materials; broad application prospects.

applicationProspects.title: Application prospects
applicationProspects.subtitle: Multi-domain application potential based on sp²–sp³ platform technology
applicationProspects.card1Desc: All-carbon composites have broad application space in high thermal conductivity heat sinks, flexible thermal pads, and solid-state battery anode materials. sp²–sp³ structure provides excellent thermal conduction while meeting porous structure and conductivity requirements for energy storage materials.
applicationProspects.viewThermal: View thermal management & energy storage applications
applicationProspects.card2Desc: As an environmentally friendly material, all-carbon composites can be used for water treatment particle electrodes and electrocatalytic applications. More frontier: high-pressure-induced internal stress may achieve high conductivity or even superconductivity, providing a new material platform for frontier research.

manufacturing.title: Preparation process
manufacturing.step1Desc: Heat nanodiamond in inert atmosphere or vacuum to a certain temperature; control time so surface of specific thickness undergoes graphitization to form graphitized shell. Core retains diamond structure.
manufacturing.step2Desc: Mix graphitized nanodiamond with graphene sheets, carbon fiber, carbon nanotubes and other sp² carbon materials at design ratio. Can use solution dispersion, mechanical mixing, or layer-by-layer assembly for uniform distribution.
manufacturing.step2Item1: Graphene approach: for rigid high thermal conductivity materials
manufacturing.step2Item2: Carbon fiber approach: for flexible thermal pads
manufacturing.step2Item3: Porous carbon approach: with pore-forming agent for solid-state batteries
manufacturing.step3Desc: In vacuum or inert atmosphere hot-press furnace, heat to specific temperature, apply pressure, hold. Graphitized shell and sp² carbon materials undergo carbon atom diffusion, forming C–C covalent bonds.
manufacturing.step4Desc: Control cool to room temperature to obtain sp²–sp³ interconnected all-carbon composite. Can perform cutting, polishing, pore formation, functionalization per application. For solid-state battery, further etch pore-forming agent to form porous structure.

patents.title: Related patent technology
patents.subtitle: Complete intellectual property protection and technology reserve
patents.patent1Title: A high thermal conductivity composite based on diamond in-situ low-temperature graphitization bonding and its preparation method and application
patents.patent1Desc: Describes in detail nanodiamond surface graphitization, mixing with graphene/carbon fiber, high-temperature pressure sintering. Covers various embodiments for thermal management material applications.
patents.patent2Title: A 3D sp² carbon skeleton reinforced flexible composite based on diamond low-temperature catalytic bonding and its preparation method
patents.patent2Desc: Uses diamond's low-temperature catalytic effect to achieve 3D covalent bonding of sp² carbon network below traditional graphitization temperature; resulting flexible composite achieves breakthrough in electrical/thermal conductivity and mechanical reinforcement efficiency.
patents.patent3Title: A high-modulus porous carbon host material and its preparation method and application
patents.patent3Desc: Specifically for solid-state battery anode applications; porous sp²–sp³ structure design including pore-forming agent selection, porosity control, electrochemical performance optimization.
patents.patent4Title: An integrated partitioned functionalized skeleton structure, all-solid-state battery and their preparation methods
patents.patent4Desc: Integrated all-solid-state battery design based on sp²–sp³ skeleton; achieves integrated co-sintering of cathode, anode, separator; significantly simplifies process.
patents.patent5Title: An sp²–sp³ interconnected all-carbon composite with chemically bonded endogenous stress and its preparation method and application
patents.patent5Desc: Focuses on internal stress structure formed under high pressure and possible superconducting properties. Covers high-end functional materials and frontier research.

investmentValue.cooperationDesc: We welcome various forms of cooperation with industrial capital, research institutions, and application enterprises. Can provide technology licensing, joint R&D, custom development; jointly promote industrialization of all-carbon composite technology.

diamondTools.title: Superabrasive fusion-bonded tools solutions
diamondTools.subtitle: (Superabrasive Fusion-Bonded Solutions)
diamondTools.desc: Based on interfacial chemical metallurgy principles, active fusion bonding achieves atomic-level reliable connection between diamond or cubic boron nitride (CBN) and heterogeneous metal substrates. We go beyond single tools; through precise matching of substrate–superabrasive–fusion alloy, we provide customized precision machining and material removal solutions for aerospace, semiconductor, and advanced composites.

hero.title: Engineering foundation: Superabrasive fusion-bonding technology
hero.subtitle: Superabrasive Brazing Technology
hero.intro: Based on over 10 years of deep research on diamond and cubic boron nitride (CBN) interface wettability, we have mastered the core "superabrasive fusion-bonding" technology. This is the engineering foundation of the sp³–sp² platform technology, achieving atomic-level chemical bonding between superabrasives and metal substrates through active fusion bonding, providing precision machining solutions for extreme working conditions worldwide.

coreCapabilities.title: Core capabilities
coreCapabilities.subtitle: From interface modification to systems engineering, building the underlying technical foundation of the sp³–sp² platform
coreCapabilities.card1Desc: Focused on solving the bonding challenge between superabrasives (Diamond/CBN) and heterogeneous substrates, using active fusion bonding (Active Fusion-Bonding) to form high-strength interface compounds for atomic-level chemical bonding.
coreCapabilities.card2Desc: Providing customized cutting/grinding solutions for "difficult-to-machine materials" such as aerospace superalloys, carbon fiber composites (CFRP), and ceramic matrix composites (CMC), ensuring process stability and efficient machining.
coreCapabilities.card3Desc: Translating industrial-grade precision control into underlying process specifications supporting sp³–sp² consumer products (e.g., diamond coating cookware) and industrial thermal management (diamond-copper), enabling continuous technology evolution.

designPrinciples.title: Product design principles: Three-element architecture of superabrasive fusion bonding
designPrinciples.subtitle: Performance of active fusion-bonded superabrasive tools is determined by three core elements: Substrate, Superabrasive, and Active fusion alloy
designPrinciples.substrateDesc: As the abrasive carrier, provides structural support and rigidity for the tool through geometric conformal adaptation to specific machining conditions.
designPrinciples.superabrasiveDesc: As micro-cutting tools, undertake material removal (Diamond/CBN), determining tool cutting capability and service life.
designPrinciples.alloyDesc: As the bonding link, determines overall tool service performance through chemical metallurgical reactions; the key control point for performance.
designPrinciples.diagram2Alt: Brazed diamond tool development factors and relationships
designPrinciples.designLogicDesc: Tool design is not simple element stacking, but comprehensive optimization based on machining object (e.g., superalloys, composites) and working conditions (dry/wet cutting). We perform full-system matching across four dimensions: substrate structure, abrasive selection, placement process, and active fusion atmosphere.
designPrinciples.logic1: Substrate–active fusion alloy compatibility: wettability, thermal expansion match
designPrinciples.logic2: Superabrasive–active fusion alloy bonding: interface chemical bonding strength
designPrinciples.logic3: Machining object material properties: hardness, brittleness, thermal sensitivity
designPrinciples.logic4: Machining condition effects: cooling method, grinding/cutting mode

extremeConditions.title: Engineering support under extreme conditions
extremeConditions.subtitle: In aerospace, semiconductor manufacturing, defense, and other fields, difficult-to-machine materials impose stringent requirements on tool reliability
extremeConditions.cmcDesc: Core material for aero-engine hot-section components; extremely difficult to machine, demanding high wear resistance and thermal stability from tools.
extremeConditions.cfrpDesc: Mainstream material for aerospace structures and new energy vehicle bodies; prone to delamination and burrs during machining, requiring precise cutting control.
extremeConditions.tiDesc: Key material for aerospace and medical devices; generates high heat during machining, posing dual challenges for tool heat dissipation and strength.
extremeConditions.valueDesc: Our solution provides not only tools but also material-matched machining process specifications. Through superabrasive fusion-bonding technology, we address chipping, thermal damage, and efficiency bottlenecks in machining high-value complex parts.
extremeConditions.processItem1: Complete cutting parameter recommendations (speed, feed, cooling method)
extremeConditions.processItem2: Tool selection guide for specific materials
extremeConditions.lifecycleItem1: On-site technical support and process optimization
extremeConditions.lifecycleItem2: Tool performance tracking and continuous improvement

fusionBonding.title: Active fusion-bonding technology deep dive
fusionBonding.principleDesc: Using customized fusion alloys in ultra-clean vacuum or controlled protective atmosphere, active components drive atomic-level chemical bonding with surface elements of superhard materials (diamond, CBN, PCD). This is not merely physical connection but atomic-level deep coupling, aimed at building high-strength, low-thermal-resistance integrated engineering structures.
fusionBonding.envNote: Effectively suppresses component volatilization, ensures fusion layer density
fusionBonding.adv1Desc: Fusion alloy optimized for superhard materials; achieves extremely high bonding strength while avoiding excessive erosion, fully preserving physicochemical properties of superabrasives.
fusionBonding.adv2Desc: Vacuum environment not only prevents oxidation but, through negative pressure precision control, ensures fusion alloy composition stability, achieving ultimate tool surface cleanliness and consistency.
fusionBonding.adv3Desc: Compared to electroplating and traditional brazing, active fusion-bonding gives stronger grain exposure, more cutting edges, significantly improving material removal rate.
fusionBonding.adv4Desc: Fusion alloy uses atomization and multi-stage screening; precisely paired with different abrasive specifications, excellent surface consistency, designed for precision and ultra-precision machining.
fusionBonding.alloy2: Adapts to various complex fusion processes
fusionBonding.substrate3: Low-carbon alloy steel and other engineering materials
fusionBonding.applicationsIntro: Active fusion-bonded tools are widely used in:

techMigration.title: Technology migration: From "interface bonding" to "all-carbon platform"
techMigration.subtitle: How the core principle of superabrasive fusion-bonding evolves into the sp³–sp² all-carbon composite platform
techMigration.originDesc: Superabrasive active fusion mechanism—achieving chemical bonding between Diamond and metal, breaking through performance limits of traditional physical setting.
techMigration.extensionDesc: Diamond-copper combination—through high-strength, low-thermal-resistance interface, building diamond-copper heat spreader substrates, entering thermal management.
techMigration.evolutionDesc: sp³ diamond and sp² carbon material homogeneous-heterogeneous bonding—breaking metal limits, building all-carbon composite platform, exploring physical computing potential.

hero.title: About Us
hero.subtitle: Originating from superabrasive tools, we have pivoted to high-performance thermal management materials. From superhard tool designers to carbon-based composite innovators, we are committed to being our customers' ideal solution provider.

overview.title: CuFeng / ToSpike: Defining the physical boundaries of carbon-based interfaces
overview.companyName: Nanjing CuFeng Mechanical & Electrical Technology Co., LTD
overview.location: Located in Jiangbei New Area, Nanjing
overview.profile1: CuFeng (ToSpike) is dedicated to carbon-based interface engineering. We leverage deep understanding of sp³–sp² hybrid bonding mechanisms to build a complete technology chain from atomic-level active fusion bonding to high-dimensional carbon-based composite platforms.
overview.profile2: We do not merely produce superabrasive tools (Diamond/CBN); we are committed to providing solutions for heat dissipation, structural strength, and electromagnetic protection in semiconductor manufacturing, aerospace, AI computing, and new energy systems, among other cutting-edge fields.
overview.rdDesc: The company has over a decade of technical accumulation in vacuum interface metallurgy, active fusion bonding, and engineering applications. Through microscopic control of the active fusion bonding process, we have overcome processing bottlenecks for cast metals, carbon fiber composites (CFRP), and hard brittle materials in extreme manufacturing, providing vertical integration solutions from custom tools to frontier functional materials.
overview.evolution: From Industrial Practice to Physics Exploration
overview.evolution1: We adhere to the "new technology, new peak" evolution path. By migrating superabrasive tool design principles to high-performance thermal management, CuFeng is achieving strategic elevation from "precision machining tools" to "high-performance functional material platform."
overview.evolution2: Through endogenous stress regulation based on sp³–sp² chemical bonding, we have achieved a material breakthrough with thermal conductivity ≥ 680 W/m·K, and continue to explore the infinite possibilities of carbon-based structures in superconductivity, unconventional transport, and quantum physics frontiers.
overview.visionDesc: Committed to being our customers' ideal solution provider, delivering breakthrough material solutions for high-power-density thermal management, new energy, and solid-state batteries through sustained technological innovation.
overview.coreIdeaDesc: New technology, new peak. Driven by technological innovation, we continuously break through materials science boundaries from superabrasive tools to carbon-based composite platforms, partnering with customers to climb new industry peaks.

wang.name: Wang Bo
wang.title: Head of Technical R&D & Applications
wang.backgroundDesc: Earned PhD in Engineering in 2016; long-term focus on precision machining research of superabrasive tools for difficult-to-machine materials.
wang.credentialsDesc: Deep industrialization experience; over 30 invention and utility model patents applied for and granted.
wang.capabilityDesc1: Expert in providing customized system solutions for extreme conditions including titanium alloys, nickel-based superalloys, silicon carbide, and carbon fiber composites (CFRP), through deep matching of sp³–sp² interface parameters.
wang.capabilityDesc2: Expert in cost-oriented, volume-oriented product development and strategic planning; achieving universal application through process improvement or paradigm innovation.

he.name: He Jun
he.title: Head of Engineering Design & Structure
he.backgroundDesc1: Master of Mechanical Engineering, Senior Engineer; 17+ years of defense electronics R&D experience, focused on structural design and thermal management.
he.backgroundDesc2: Long-term tenure at major defense electronics research institutions; led multiple high-complexity electronic equipment structural R&D and manufacturing projects in defense and civilian sectors.
he.capabilityDesc1: Expert in integrated structure–thermal coupling design for complex electronic equipment; full-chain capability from system design to engineering implementation.
he.capabilityDesc2: Deep understanding of thermal design for high-power-density electronic equipment; provides reliable technical paths and innovative solutions.
he.capabilityDesc3: Rich engineering practice and problem-closure experience; adept at balancing performance, reliability, and process feasibility under constraints.

zhang.name: Zhang Gong
zhang.title: Head of Application Engineering & Field Support
zhang.backgroundDesc: Senior diamond tool application expert; years of experience in superabrasive tools and material cutting mechanisms. Expert in solving complex industrial grinding challenges; dedicated to innovative application and market promotion of high-end new energy vehicle assembly tools.
zhang.solutionDesc: Deep dive into on-site pain points; full-chain support from tool selection to process parameter optimization.
zhang.responseDesc: Ensure seamless integration of technical support and production; significantly improve line efficiency and stability.
zhang.evAssemblyDesc: For three-electric systems (battery, motor, controller) and chassis assembly; provide high-precision, high-durability dedicated tool solutions.

timeline.title: Core Milestones
timeline.subtitle: Key milestones from launch to technological breakthrough
timeline.2025Patents: Patent cluster and technology barrier formation
timeline.2025PatentsDesc: Completed 9 core patent layouts, building a complete IP moat from diamond tools to sp³–sp² carbon-based interface.
timeline.2025ThermalDesc: Successfully developed diamond-copper composite with thermal conductivity reaching 680 W/m·K, marking the company's formal entry into high-power electronic thermal management.

timeline.2018FoundingDesc: Company registered in Zhenjiang, establishing the "new technology, new peak" philosophy and focusing on solving interface connection challenges for difficult-to-machine materials.

applications.title: Application Domain Matrix
applications.subtitle: Based on sp³–sp² carbon hybrid bonding platform technology, we apply the same core methodology to different physical scenarios, forming a complete technology matrix from industrial thermal management to frontier physics exploration.

blueprint.title: Application Blueprint: From Underlying Physics to Full-Scenario Deployment

blueprint.layer1Desc: Addressing thermal bottlenecks under extreme power density, providing 500–800 W/m·K efficient thermal solutions.
blueprint.layer2Items: IGBT modules|Laser thermal management|5G base stations|Phased array radar

evStorage.items: Power battery thermal management structural components|Motor controller thermal interface substrate|On-board charger thermal management solution|DC/DC converter heat spreader
evStorage.itemsDesc: High-thermal-conductivity carbon composite as inter-module TIM or cold plate contact layer improves pack thermal uniformity and reduces thermal runaway risk.|Diamond-copper or flexible TIM between IGBT/SiC modules and heat sink significantly reduces contact resistance and improves power density.|OBC power devices have high heat flux; diamond-copper heat spreaders enable rapid heat spreading and reduce system weight and volume.|DC/DC converter multi-chip layout requires high-thermal-conductivity TIM and heat spreaders for coordinated multi-source heat dissipation.
evStorage.materials: Diamond-copper composite|Flexible thermal pad|All-carbon sp²–sp³ composite

consumer.title: Consumer Electronics & Smart Home
consumer.items: High-power wireless charging high-thermal-conductivity coils (11kW+)|Diamond coating nonstick cookware|Foldable phone ultra-thin thermal management|VR/AR device lightweight thermal management
consumer.itemsDesc: Diamond-copper composite conductor builds radial heat channels on copper wire surface, addressing ACR surge and thermal bottlenecks from skin effect for 11kW+ wireless charging.|sp³–sp² chemical bonding firmly anchors diamond particles on cookware surface for thermal + wear + nonstick integration, far exceeding traditional coating lifetime.|Ultra-thin flexible TIM conforms to foldable hinge and hot chip areas for efficient heat spreading in limited space, ensuring fold reliability.|Lightweight thermal solutions replace metal heat sinks, enabling reliable cooling for key chips under VR/AR headset weight constraints.
consumer.materials: Diamond-copper composite|Diamond coating/bonding material|Flexible high-thermal-conductivity composite
consumer.expandLabel: View wireless charging coil solution details
consumer.coilIntro: For 11kW/22kW+ wireless charging coils, addressing ACR surge and thermal bottlenecks caused by skin effect and proximity effect from high-frequency currents.
consumer.coilSolution: Diamond-copper composite conductor technology (electroplating + medium-temperature diffusion) using 20μm diamond and pure copper to build ultra-high thermal conductivity, low thermal resistance radial heat dissipation channels.
consumer.coilAdv1: Conductivity up to 100% IACS, significantly reducing coil heating (20%+ reduction)
consumer.coilAdv2: Flexible structure suitable for small-radius winding
consumer.coilMech: Multi-level filler network of diamond + AlN/Al₂O₃ greatly dilutes low-thermal-conductivity resin content, achieving Ultra-Low R_th effect.
consumer.viewWhitepaper: Download wireless charging coil thermal whitepaper

frontier.title: New Energy Storage & Frontier Exploration
frontier.sectionSubtitle: Based on the same sp³–sp² carbon bonding platform, this module spans the full technology spectrum from foreseeable engineering extension to frontier physics exploration. Solid-state battery anodes and water treatment particle electrodes build on mature process paths with clear medium-term engineering potential; superconducting material research and quantum mesoscopic systems rely on the stress engineering platform and are in collaborative frontier exploration with academia. Together they demonstrate the platform's extensibility.
frontier.mediumTermSubtitle: Foreseeable engineering extension based on sp³–sp² platform
frontier.longTermSubtitle: Frontier exploration based on stress engineering, collaborative research stage
frontier.items: Solid-state battery anode architecture (high-modulus carbon host)|Water treatment particle electrodes|Superconducting material fundamental research (exploration)|Quantum mesoscopic system vision (long-term planning)
frontier.itemsDesc: sp³–sp² carbon skeleton as high-modulus host suppresses lithium dendrite growth via stress field, improving solid-state battery cycle life and safety.|High internal stress sp²–sp³ structure delivers high hardness, long life, and excellent electrical/catalytic activity for electrochemical water treatment electrodes.|Based on sp³–sp² interface band distortion under high stress, probing unconventional electronic transport for superconducting research.|All-carbon network lattice distortion regulates coherent electron behavior, exploring stress engineering evolution in quantum computing candidate materials.
frontier.materials: High-modulus carbon host|sp²–sp³ electrochemical electrode|All-carbon composite platform

thermal.title: Core Industrial Thermal Management Solutions
thermal.subtitle: Addressing thermal management bottlenecks for high-power-density electronic devices, providing thermal interface substrate solutions beyond traditional copper/aluminum limits
thermal.aiChipTitle: AI High-Density Chip Thermal Management Solution
thermal.aiChipDesc: As AI compute chip power density continues to increase, thermal management has become one of the bottlenecks limiting technological breakthroughs. Based on our sp³–sp² chemical bonding material platform, we propose a thermal management solution that breaks through traditional microchannel liquid cooling, effectively addressing high-density thermal management challenges in supercomputing and laser systems.
thermal.geoCouplingDesc: Innovative 3D conformal surface design that precisely fits chip packaging to minimize interface thermal resistance
thermal.thermalDilutionDesc: Combining the excellent phonon thermal conductivity of diamond-copper and all-carbon composites for fast heat spreading of localized high heat flux
thermal.systemSyncDesc: Works in synergy with in-house high-thermal-conductivity TIM materials to deliver long-life, zero-risk thermal management solutions
thermal.scenario1: AI chips (GPU/NPU) ultra-high power density thermal management
thermal.scenario3: Supercomputing center data center liquid cooling systems
thermal.scenario4: Edge computing unit high-density thermal management
thermal.powerSemiconductorDesc: Reduce SiC/GaN device junction temperature, significantly improve system power density and reliability
thermal.psScenario2: High-speed rail inverter thermal management solution
thermal.psScenario3: Industrial inverter power module thermal interface
thermal.psScenario4: PV/energy storage inverter thermal management solution
thermal.evDesc: Address thermal runaway risk in battery packs and electric control systems, reduce system weight
thermal.evScenario1: Power battery thermal management structural components
thermal.fiveGDesc: Address local heat flux density management for high-frequency high-power devices
thermal.fiveGScenario1: 5G base station power amplifier (PA) thermal management
thermal.fiveGScenario3: Satellite communication ground station RF units

energy.sectionSubtitle: Based on mature process platforms, committed to solving electrochemical efficiency challenges in energy storage and water treatment
energy.solidStatePhysicsDesc: Using endogenous stress regulation of sp³–sp² carbon skeleton as high-modulus carbon host material, stress field suppresses lithium dendrite growth and improves high-modulus anode stability.

energy.solidStateAdv2: sp³–sp² chemical bonding structure provides excellent structural stability
energy.solidStateAdv3: Stress regulation mechanism effectively suppresses dendrite growth

energy.waterAdv3: Catalytic activity enhances pollutant degradation capability

frontier.sectionSubtitle: Evolution potential of platform technology in extreme physics and future computing
frontier.superconductorPhysicsDesc: Based on possible band structure distortion of sp³–sp² interfaces under high internal stress fields, probing whether unconventional electronic transport properties exist at extremely low temperatures or special energy level matching.
frontier.superconductorRes1: Observe stress gradient effects on interface electron localization
frontier.superconductorRes2: Explore interface nonlinear conductance under non-von Neumann structures
frontier.superconductorRes3: Collaborate with external labs for cryogenic transport property surveys
frontier.superconductorVision: As a new carbon-based material platform, providing a possible experimental substrate for unconventional superconducting mechanism research.
frontier.quantumPhysicsDesc: By precisely regulating lattice distortion of all-carbon networks, attempt to study coherent electron evolution behavior in stress-field-induced heterogeneous potential, evaluating preliminary feasibility as quantum computing candidate material.
frontier.quantumRes1: Theoretical modeling of interface coherent electron behavior
frontier.quantumRes3: Preliminary analysis of electron decoherence factors at mesoscopic scale
frontier.quantumVision: Aiming to explore evolution potential of stress engineering at extreme microscopic scale, as theoretical and technical reserve for computing architecture transformation in the next decade.

summary.title: Systematic Output of Underlying Logic
summary.tagline: One starting point, multi-dimensional collapse, infinite engineering boundaries
summary.desc: Every application domain here is not isolated product development, but a "collapse" result of the core proposition of controlled sp³–sp² interface energy levels at different physical scales. From extreme thermal management for 1000W+ instantaneous thermal shock to physical performance breakthroughs across interface thermal resistance, we consistently use the same underlying bonding logic to resolve performance bottlenecks across industries.

hero.title: Technical Dialogue & Strategic Alignment
hero.subtitle: Technical Dialogue & Strategic Alignment
hero.p1: We do not regard this project as a fully defined, mature product ready for replication or scaling.
hero.p2: It is closer to a material and physics platform in evolution—
hero.p3: originating from engineering practice, but gradually evolving potential space across materials science, functional materials, and fundamental physics exploration.
hero.p4: Therefore, the purpose of this page is not to promote specific cooperation models,
hero.p5: but to declare a technical state that can be understood and aligned.

platform.title: An evolving platform, not a single-point technology
platform.content: What this project builds is not a single material, single device, or single application, but a complete carbon-based material system centered on sp³–sp² chemical bonding and endogenous stress regulation.
platform.content2: This system has formed a clear path for material preparation and functional validation at the engineering level,
platform.content3: while at a deeper level, it presents the possibility of continued evolution as a platform.
platform.content4: We are well aware of the gap from lab to fab; therefore, while pursuing physical limits, we maintain rigorous respect for process reproducibility and industrial boundaries.
platform.content5: This means it can be used to solve real engineering problems,
platform.content6: or may in the future be incorporated into higher-level systematic research and application frameworks.

audience.title: Who we are open to engaging with
audience.intro: At this stage, we focus more on cognitive alignment than on formal cooperation arrangements.
audience.intro2: Therefore, we are open to dialogue with the following types of institutions or individuals:
audience.research: Research institutions with long-term interest in material physics, interface structure, and multi-scale evolution
audience.industry: Industry players seeking next-generation functional materials or foundational material platforms, willing to co-define product boundaries in application scenarios
audience.tech: Technical teams with cross-disciplinary judgment, capable of understanding "platform value" rather than single-point product value
audience.investor: Strategic investors focused on medium-to-long-term technology inflection points, with patience and independent judgment
audience.footnote: We do not seek broad coverage; we care more that discussions occur from the same technical perspective.

nextSteps.title: On further advancement
nextSteps.p1: Under the right cognitive premise, there can be multiple possibilities for advancing this technology system.
nextSteps.p2: These possibilities do not presuppose specific forms or predetermined paths,
nextSteps.p3: but depend on the match between both parties in technical understanding, development pace, and long-term goals.
nextSteps.p4: Before that, any formal definition would be premature.

resonance.title: A resonance, not mere recruitment
resonance.p1: We do not expect to complete all judgments through a single web browse.
resonance.p2: If the physical logic described herein resonates with your intuition about the evolution of future materials,
resonance.p3: if you likewise believe that breakthroughs in underlying materials are the ultimate key to transcending industry cycles and solving thermal and efficiency bottlenecks,
resonance.p4: then we may already stand on a similar technical perspective.
resonance.p5: What we look forward to is this kind of cognitive convergence across time and space.

cooperation.quote: The true value of technology often lies not in the moment it is named, but in the space it preserves before it is understood.

hero.title: Technology Architecture & Evolution Path
hero.subtitle: Underlying materials science platform based on sp³–sp² interface engineering
hero.desc: We have built a complete carbon-based, atomic-level interface engineering platform, closing the loop from theoretical hypothesis to physical validation, and an airtight intellectual property protection network of 10 core patents. Through precise control of the sp³–sp² hybrid bonding network, we have achieved material performance leaps in the following three dimensions:
hero.dimensions: · Interface Engineering | · High-Performance Thermal Management | · Extreme Condition Response

architecture.title: Technology Deepening Path & Platform Architecture Overview
architecture.stage01Desc: Establishing interface connection and structural stability engineering capability under extreme conditions
architecture.stage02Desc: Achieving the leap from engineering tools to functional materials
architecture.stage03Desc: Building a composite material platform entirely composed of carbon systems

evolution.sectionTitle: Cross-Domain Cohesive Knowledge Chain: From Material Properties to Computing Core
evolution.sectionSubtitle: An evolution path from engineering tools to physical computing potential
evolution.coreLogicDesc: Through stress regulation of sp³–sp² bonding structures, we evolved from “heat dissipation tools” into functional substrates with “physical computing potential.” This path not only validates technical feasibility, but also reveals the vast imagination space at the intersection of materials science and computational physics

evolutionPath.sectionSubtitle: Systematic evolution from engineering tools to all-carbon material systems
evolutionPath.stage01Desc: Centered on active fusion-bonded diamond tools, we have established engineering capability for diamond interface, high-temperature bonding, and structural stability under extreme conditions.
evolutionPath.funcExtensionDescBefore: Based on interface and wear-resistant technology accumulated in the active fusion-bonded diamond tool stage, related capabilities have been extended to civilian functional scenarios requiring higher wear resistance, stability, and safety—
evolutionPath.funcExtensionDescAfter: are a functional extension application of this stage.
evolutionPath.patent1Title: A novel nonstick cookware and its preparation method
evolutionPath.patent2Title: A novel nonstick cookware based on diamond bonding
evolutionPath.stage02Desc: Introducing metallic phase to form composite materials, achieving the leap from engineering tools to functional materials, focusing on thermal conductivity, structural composite, and interface transport capability.
evolutionPath.techBranchDesc: Patent layout at this stage covers both low-cost solutions for scaled applications and high-performance solutions for high-end thermal management and extreme performance needs, forming a complementary technical portfolio.
evolutionPath.typicalFormsDescAfter: are typical functional forms of the diamond-copper composite stage, aiming to convert material high thermal conductivity and structural stability into engineerable thermal management solutions.
evolutionPath.thermalPadSubstrate: thermal pads and heat spreader substrates
evolutionPath.patent3Title: A diamond/copper composite material and its preparation method
evolutionPath.patent4Title: A structured diamond thermal management substrate and its preparation method
evolutionPath.patent5Title: A flexible high-thermal-conductivity composite reinforced by active fusion-bonding metal skeleton and its preparation method
evolutionPath.stage03Desc: Through demetallization process, building a composite system centered on continuous sp³–sp² carbon network, providing underlying architecture for complex stress regulation and higher-order physical behavior exploration.
evolutionPath.formDescText: The all-carbon composite stage covers both structural and flexible forms to meet different thermal management and structural integration needs.
evolutionPath.patent6Title: A high-thermal-conductivity composite based on diamond in-situ low-temperature graphitization bonding and its preparation method and application
evolutionPath.patent7Title: A 3D SP2 carbon skeleton reinforced flexible composite based on diamond low-temperature catalytic bonding and its preparation method
evolutionPath.patent8Title: A high-modulus porous carbon host material and its preparation method and application
evolutionPath.patent9Title: An integrated partitioned functionalized skeleton structure, all-solid-state battery and their preparation methods
evolutionPath.patent10Title: An sp²–sp³ interconnected all-carbon composite with chemically bonded endogenous stress and its preparation method and application
evolutionPath.stage04Title: Stress Engineering Based on All-Carbon Platform
evolutionPath.stage04Desc: Based on the all-carbon composite platform, stress engineering extends from material function to the physical system level, constituting the endpoint of the evolution path and providing an experimental substrate for higher-order property exploration.
evolutionPath.stage04Explanation: The "Platform Extension" section below expands on specific exploration directions derived from this (superconductivity, chaotic dynamics, quantum reservoir computing, etc.). We are collaborating with academia to advance related research.

platformCore.sectionSubtitle: Underlying technical principles and tunable parameter space
platformCore.mainTitle: sp³–sp² Chemical Bonding and Endogenous Stress Structure
platformCore.mainDesc: The chemical bonding structure is the core kernel naturally evolved in the all-carbon composite stage. Through synergistic action of endogenous stress and chemical bonding, this structure forms a material platform with long-term stability and scalability, providing foundation for subsequent functional materials and physical exploration.
platformCore.principleP1: In the all-carbon composite system, carbon atoms can exist in different bonding modes:
platformCore.sp3Role: bonding primarily provides 3D rigid structure (similar to diamond)
platformCore.sp2Role: bonding primarily provides 2D conductive/thermal plane (similar to graphite/graphene)
platformCore.principleP2: Through high-temperature and high-pressure processes, atomic-level chemical bonding is achieved at the interface, generating a stable endogenous stress field and forming a unique
platformCore.diagramTitle: Principle illustration: different parameter combinations generate different macroscopic functional domains
platformCore.domain1Title: sp³ dominant, relatively low sp² content
platformCore.domain1a: Strong structural rigidity, high thermal stability
platformCore.domain1b: Suitable for high-strength, wear-resistant, stable-condition materials
platformCore.domain2Title: sp² dominant, relatively high sp² content
platformCore.domain2b: Suitable for heat dissipation, electronic interface coupling, transport-related functions
platformCore.domain3b: Exhibits complex interface energy states and nonlinear response behavior
platformCore.tuningIntro: These parameters are typically regulated synergistically through:
platformCore.tuningOutro: to achieve target multi-scale structural states.
platformCore.platformConclusion: This "multi-state carbon network" with endogenous stress and chemical interconnection is an advantage from an engineering functional material perspective, and from a physics exploration perspective provides a high-dimensional complex property space—this is why we call it the

platformExtension.sectionTitle: Long-term Physical Potential: Emergent Behavior of sp³–sp² Structure
platformExtension.sectionSubtitle: Based on the stress engineering platform, exploring evolution potential of all-carbon sp³–sp² structure in extreme physics and future computing
platformExtension.intro: In the demetallized all-carbon composite stage, we have built a class of continuous carbon network structures centered on sp³–sp² chemical bonding and endogenous stress fields. This structure has demonstrated excellent thermal conductivity, mechanical properties, and long-term stability at the engineering level, while at a higher level naturally opening a series of physical windows not yet fully explored.
platformExtension.superconductorTitle: Electronic State Reconstruction and Unconventional Superconducting Potential Under High Internal Stress Regulation
platformExtension.superconductorP1: Under strong endogenous stress and non-uniform bonding environments, localized electronic structure may undergo reorganization, providing structural basis for unconventional superconductivity or related collective phenomena.
platformExtension.superconductorP2: In existing material systems, carbon-based structures simultaneously possessing strong endogenous stress, continuous sp³–sp² bonding, and engineerable tuning windows remain rare, making this platform unique in fundamental structure exploration for unconventional superconducting materials.
platformExtension.chaosTitle: Chaotic Dynamics Behavior of Nonlinearly Coupled Carbon Networks
platformExtension.chaosP1: Interconnected networks form highly coupled, disordered but controllable structural units at mesoscopic scale, providing physical carrier for complex dynamics and reservoir computing-like behavior.
platformExtension.chaosP2: Based on nonlinear response of material endogenous dynamics, this network can achieve efficient temporal information encoding. Through precise regulation of chaotic states, we aim to build a physical computing paradigm without traditional transistor logic, breaking through the compute-energy-efficiency bottleneck from the ground up.
platformExtension.quantumTitle: Long-term Exploration of Quantum Reservoir Computing
platformExtension.quantumP1: Compared to condensed matter physics directions such as superconductivity, this direction is more exploratory. We are exploring a platform-based "quantum reservoir computing" model, but it is not a current primary technical target.
platformExtension.quantumP2: This platform is not directly aimed at current quantum computing architectures, but provides material-level foundation for exploring novel, non-von Neumann, physics-driven computing paradigms.
platformExtension.disclaimer: It must be emphasized that the above directions remain in the physical exploration stage and have not yet entered engineering or productization. Yet it is precisely this undeveloped physical ceiling that gives the platform potential value distinct from traditional functional materials at the long-term strategic level.
